title: grants — permissions
description: Permission strings this module publishes. Split by guard: `sanctum` for tenant admins + tenant users managing grants; `platform_admin` for Academorix ops with cross-tenant read + audit-report authority. Seeded by GrantPermissionSeeder with is_system=true.

permissions.name: grants.viewAny
permissions.description: List + show grants in the caller's tenant. Scoped by default to grants where subject_id = auth_user (users see their own grants); widened for admins with `grants.manage`.

permissions.name: grants.manage
permissions.description: Full grant CRUD scope. Includes issue, patch, revoke, view any grant in the tenant (not just own).

permissions.name: grants.issue
permissions.description: Issue a grant. The grantor must ALSO hold every permission being granted (privilege-escalation guard — enforced by observer + issuer regardless of this permission). Members with grants.issue can share access on resources they own (e.g. a coach sharing view on athletes they manage).

permissions.name: grants.revoke
permissions.description: Revoke a grant the caller issued (granted_by = auth_user). Broader revocation (any grant in the tenant) requires `grants.manage`.

permissions.name: grants.audit_any
permissions.description: Enumerate grants across the tenant — call `/grants/subject/{id}` for any principal or `/grants/resource/{type}/{id}` for any resource. Admin-level compliance surface.

permissions.name: grants.bulk_provision
permissions.description: Upload a CSV of grants for bulk issue. Gated by the `bulk_grant_provision` entitlement (medium plan+) AND this permission.

permissions.name: grants.deny
permissions.description: Issue a grant with decision='deny' (an inviolable block). Rarer than allow — split into its own permission because denies short-circuit everything and are audit-critical. Gated by the `deny_grants` entitlement (enterprise) AND this permission.

permissions.name: grants.grant_across_role
permissions.description: Rare enterprise workflow: grant a permission the grantor DOES NOT hold in their own role, via an approval flow. Requires this permission AND the `cross_role_grants` entitlement AND a matching workflow/approvals template. Without it, the privilege-escalation guard refuses.

permissions.name: platform.grants.viewAny
permissions.description: Cross-tenant list. Every read captured in audit context (SOC 2 CC6.1).

permissions.name: platform.grants.audit_report
permissions.description: Generate cross-tenant compliance audit reports. Enforced by the `grant_audit_report` entitlement AND this permission.

notes: The `grants.issue` seeded on `member` looks liberal but is neutered by the privilege-escalation guard: a member can only issue grants for permissions THEY HOLD (via their role). A member cannot grant admin-scope permissions no matter how the RBAC config drifts.

Deny grants (`grants.deny`) are a separate permission because they are inviolable — a compromised admin issuing denies is worse than an admin issuing allows. Splitting them lets tenants restrict deny-issue to a smaller role tier.

`grants.grant_across_role` is the escape hatch for the rare 'CFO delegating limited access to a consultant they don't share role permissions with' pattern. Requires approval workflow gating — the grant cannot land without a template match.
